title: activity — HTTP middleware
description: One middleware this module contributes: `activity.enforce_scope`. Filters list results to entities the caller can access (RBAC-scoped) AND starts an activity batch for the request so downstream writes carry a consistent `batch_uuid`. Also relies on framework + tenants middleware composed on its route groups.

middleware.description: Two responsibilities: (1) On every tenant-audience request, calls `ActivityBatcher::beginBatch()` — starts a request-scoped batch that any downstream `LogsActivity` write will attach to via `batch_uuid`. On response terminate, calls `ActivityBatcher::completeBatch()`. (2) On list / show requests targeting Activity endpoints, augments the query with an RBAC filter — the caller sees activities on entities they hold viewing permissions for. Uses `AccessGrantResolver` + role-based policies to compute the acceptable `subject_type + subject_id` set; queries with a `filter[subject_type]` unauthorised by the caller are rewritten to return empty.
sideEffects: Starts an activity batch keyed by (request_id, tenant_id, application_id).
Attaches a `resolveActivityQueryScope()` callback to the request that the ActivityRepository consumes on list endpoints.

On terminate, calls ActivityBatcher::completeBatch() → emits ActivityBatchCompleted (afterCommit) if activitiesWritten > 0, or discards silently if empty (empty batches don't emit — they're not interesting).

notes: The middleware is the ONLY activity-specific HTTP middleware in Wave 6. Retention + purge + broadcast live in observers + jobs, not middleware.
For platform-admin routes, batch tracking is skipped — platform mutations route to audit, not activity. The middleware detects this and short-circuits both responsibilities.

The `resolveActivityQueryScope` callback returns a closure that the ActivityRepository applies to every list query: WHERE subject_type + subject_id IN (caller's accessible entities). This is cross-cutting RBAC — activity is one of the few surfaces where a user's own view is subject-permission-narrowed.
Feature-flag `activity.batch-collapse` can turn the batch tracking off in an incident. When off, every activity row surfaces individually (no batch_uuid populated); the RBAC filter still applies.
